title: Getting a Second Shot to Stop Meningitis
summary: In Kent, a place in England, there was a sickness called meningitis B that made some people very sick and sadly two people died. To help stop this sickness, doctors gave a special shot called a vaccine to almost 12,000 people who might have been near the sickness. Now, these people will get a second shot starting next week to make sure they are protected. The shots are given at clinics in towns like Canterbury, Faversham, and Ashford. People can book their shot online. The sickness started at a nightclub and spread to university students and school pupils. The vaccine helps fight the bacteria that cause meningitis B. Getting two shots is important, with the second one given at least four weeks after the first. This is part of a bigger effort by the NHS to keep everyone safe.

the National Health Service in the UK that helps take care of people's health

background_read: Meningitis B is a dangerous infection that affects the brain and spinal cord. Vaccines are medicines that teach our bodies to fight germs. In the UK, the NHS provides healthcare for everyone. When many people get sick in one area, it's called an outbreak, and doctors work quickly to give vaccines to stop it from spreading.
Article_Structure: WHO: Almost 12,000 people in Kent, including university students and school pupils
WHAT: Getting a second dose of the MenB vaccine to protect against meningitis B
WHERE: Clinics in Canterbury, Faversham, and Ashford in Kent, England
WHY: To stop the spread of meningitis B after an outbreak that made people sick and caused two deaths

perspectives.description: The NHS is working to keep people safe by offering vaccines to prevent more sickness.
